name: Additive Agent Tool Surface Construction
summary: Merge agent-specific tool sets (MCP clients + fetched tools) into the parent context without disrupting the parent’s existing pool.
when_to_use: Use this when bringing tools from newly connected MCP servers into the agent’s tool pool while preserving the parent context.

name: Agent-Specific MCP Server Initialization
summary: Connect each agent’s frontmatter MCP servers safely and tear them down without leaking clients, while honoring plugin-only guards.
when_to_use: Load this skill when an agent defines extra MCP servers in its frontmatter and those clients must be joined to the parent session.

name: Aggregated Change Signal Return
summary: Collapse multiple change sources into one caller-facing boolean so headless bootstraps know whether a downstream refresh is necessary.
when_to_use: Apply when a headless setup flow can change state through several mechanisms but callers only need one final answer about whether they must refresh downstream registries.

name: ANSI-Safe Border Caption Embedding
summary: Embed captions into terminal borders while preserving alignment, clipping, and color/dim semantics.
when_to_use: Apply when you must insert text into a border line (top or bottom) while preserving the border characters, colors, and alignment, even if the caption is wider than available space.

name: API Round Message Grouping
summary: Split conversation history into API-round buckets so compaction retries cut only the chunks tied to the failed round.
when_to_use: When compacting or retrying requests, use API-round boundaries instead of human-turn heuristics to determine what past messages belong together.

name: API-Round PTL Retry Truncation
summary: On prompt-too-long retries, drop the oldest API-round groups first and inject a synthetic user preamble when needed so the summarize request stays valid.
when_to_use: Apply when the compaction request itself hits prompt-too-long and you need a last-resort retry path that removes old context without breaking API message validity.

name: Append-Only Sidechain Transcripts
summary: Persist subagent transcripts as an append-only ordered log so forks, resumes, and sidechain readers can replay history without duplication.
when_to_use: Subagent transcript persistence must stay append-only so forked history can be resumed and sidechain readers see messages in strict chronological order.

name: Atomic Post-Await State Commit
summary: After awaiting persistence work, update the shared budget state for each `tool_use_id` exactly once so no concurrent reader ever sees a half-committed decision.
when_to_use: Use this pattern when concurrently persisting large tool results and mutating `ContentReplacementState` once the awaited work completes, such as the `freshReplacements` loop inside `enforceToolResultBudget`.

name: Auto Memory Tool Permission Fencing
summary: Gate every tool call in the auto-memory extract agent so only safe read-only actions and targeted writes execute.
when_to_use: Apply whenever a background auto-memory agent must be limited to safe read-only commands and writes that target the memory directory.

name: Auto Refresh After New Plugin Sources
summary: Clear source and plugin caches, then trigger a best-effort refresh whenever reconciliation installs new sources.
when_to_use: Use when newly installed plugin sources should become live immediately and the runtime must attempt an automatic refresh before falling back to manual recovery.

name: Autocompact Circuit Breaker
summary: Break the autocompact retry loop after several consecutive failures to avoid hammering shared APIs.
when_to_use: Apply when autocompact failures repeat and the service must stop retrying to avoid API storms.

name: Autocompact Recursion Guards
summary: Block autocompact from running in recursive contexts so forks and helpers don’t deadlock.
when_to_use: Apply when auto-compacting logic runs inside recursive agents (session memory, compact helpers, context collapse) and must avoid deadlocking by re-entering itself.

name: Background Marketplace Reconciliation
summary: Diff declared plugin marketplaces against local state, seed pending UI rows, and reconcile them in the background without blocking startup.
when_to_use: Use when plugin sources or marketplaces may need install or update work at startup, but the main agent loop must stay responsive while the UI still shows per-source status.

name: Bridge Origin Safe Slash Override
summary: Keep remote slash-command skipping enabled by default, then reopen it only for bridge-safe commands while denying unsafe local commands with a friendly local response.
when_to_use: Apply when remotely delivered input should still default to plain-text slash handling, but a trusted bridge path needs to selectively re-enable only the slash commands that are safe to execute remotely.

name: Bridge Safe Command Advertising
summary: Advertise only slash commands that a bridge client can actually invoke by filtering announcements with the same bridge-safety predicate used at execution time.
when_to_use: Use when a bridged or remote client receives an announced slash-command list and that list must stay aligned with the commands the runtime will actually execute for bridge-originated input.

name: Budget-Aware Skill Listing Formatting
summary: Derive the active model context budget at runtime and format skill listings to fit it instead of emitting raw command sets.
when_to_use: Apply when a skill or command listing must fit the active model's real context budget instead of assuming a fixed token allowance.

name: Budget Diminishing Continue
summary: Issue continuation nudges while the turn stays under budget, then halt once diminishing returns or the budget cap justify stopping.
when_to_use: Apply when you have a task-level token budget and must decide between continuing and stopping while respecting diminishing returns.

name: Built-In Agent Feature Gating
summary: Expose built-in agents according to entrypoint, mode, and feature flags so each runtime gets the right built-in surface.
when_to_use: Use when built-in agent registration should differ across CLI, SDK, coordinator, or experiment-enabled entrypoints.

name: Bundled-and-MCP Turn-Zero Skill Filter
summary: Shrink first-turn skill listings to bundled and MCP-backed high-signal sources, with a deterministic bundled-only fallback if still too large.
when_to_use: Use when turn-zero skill listings must stay inside a tight prompt budget without losing the most useful high-signal skills.

name: Bundled Skill File Extraction
summary: Lazily extract referenced files for bundled skills so large prompts stay offline until needed.
when_to_use: Use whenever a bundled skill needs reference files extracted on demand while keeping startup fast.

name: Cache Edit Pin and Replay
summary: Queue cache edits once, pin them to message positions, and replay them across turns instead of regenerating them on every retry.
when_to_use: Use when cache-editing or microcompact flows must preserve deletion edits across retries, resumes, or later turns.

name: Cache Identical Fork Prefix Construction
summary: Build forked conversation prefixes that stay identical across turns so prompt caching keeps working.
when_to_use: Use this when a forked agent must reuse the parent prompt cache and needs byte-identical prefixes for each invocation.

name: Cache Prefix Stability For Subagents
summary: Preserve cache-critical request fields when a child agent must share the parent's prompt prefix for cache hits.
when_to_use: Apply when a subagent should share the parent's cached prompt prefix instead of recomputing a divergent request shape.

name: Cache-Safe Stop-Hook Snapshotting
summary: Persist a cache-safe stop-hook context snapshot only for main session queries so later helpers can resume from stable state without fork pollution.
when_to_use: Apply when end-of-turn hooks need to persist enough state for follow-up helpers, but background forks must not overwrite the main session snapshot.

name: Cache Sweep Activation Fallback
summary: Sweep stale extension caches before activation, then fall back to a sticky manual-refresh flag if the live refresh fails.
when_to_use: Apply when newly materialized extension sources must be activated immediately, but the live refresh can fail and needs a recoverable fallback.

name: Cache Warm Before Cache-Only Fanout
summary: Warm the authoritative cache path first, then fan out cache-only readers in parallel so derived loaders never race stale state.
when_to_use: Apply when one authoritative load populates caches that several cheaper readers depend on, and parallel reads would race the warm path.

name: Canonical-Root Marketplace Path Normalization
summary: Normalize relative extension source paths against a canonical repo root so shared records stay stable across worktrees.
when_to_use: Use when local extension paths are stored in a shared registry and multiple worktrees or checkouts might otherwise stamp conflicting absolute paths.

name: Canonical Worktree Path Normalization
summary: Resolve repo-relative paths against the canonical checkout root so shared state stays stable across Git worktrees.
when_to_use: Use when repo-relative paths are written into shared or user-global state and the same project may be opened from multiple worktrees.

name: Capability-Aware Offload Skipping
summary: Skip generic output offload for tools whose own contracts already bound output or would become circular if wrapped again.
when_to_use: Use when a generic persistence or preview wrapper covers many tools but some tools should bypass it because they already self-bound output or would recurse.

name: Capability Based Spinner Glyph Selection
summary: Select spinner glyphs and colors dynamically based on terminal capability, motion preference, and stall state.
when_to_use: Use when building a spinner glyph that must adapt to reduced-motion settings, theme colors, and stalled intensity while keeping the animation loop small.

name: Capability Pool Resolution
summary: Resolve an agent’s tool intent and disallowed lists into the actual pool the agent can use, honoring wildcard, async, and teammate allowances.
when_to_use: Apply this pattern when an agent declares requested tools/disallowedTools and you must compute the exact capabilities available to that agent thread.

name: Case-Normalized Sensitive Path Guards
summary: Normalize filesystem paths and reject matches that try to spoof sensitive directories with mixed case or alternate separators.
when_to_use: Use this whenever user input targets `.claude`, dotfiles, or other dangerous locations on case-insensitive filesystems (Windows/macos).

name: Circular-Safe Tool Registry Loading
summary: Safely assemble the tool registry when optional tools have circular dependencies or heavy side effects.
when_to_use: Use when tools reference each other (e.g., `TeamCreateTool` ↔ `tools.ts`) or only exist under feature flags, and you still need a single exhaustive registry.

name: Classified Skill Listing Debug Telemetry
summary: Emit low-cost debug telemetry for skill-listing batches that records batch size, initial-vs-dynamic mode, and cumulative sent count without logging the payload body.
when_to_use: Apply when a listing or announcement pipeline needs debuggable operational signals, but logging the full payload would be noisy, expensive, or sensitive.

name: CLI Command Surface Audit
summary: Audit a command surface end to end so registration, discoverability, permissions, and execution all line up before shipping.
when_to_use: Use when adding, migrating, or debugging commands or slash-command skills in a coding CLI with multiple registries or mode filters.

name: Compact Boundary Session Distillation
summary: Distill the compact boundary delta into the session memory summary and the freshest user cues needed for skill capture prompts.
when_to_use: When skillify captures a repeating process, use the latest session memory and the user messages after the most recent compact boundary so the prompt only contains the current instructions.

name: Compactable Tool Whitelist Gating
summary: Only allow cached microcompact to delete tool results for a vetted, pre-gated whitelist of tool names.
when_to_use: Apply when cache-editing compacts conversation transcripts and must avoid deleting outputs from tools that cannot be safely re-run or are non-text.

name: Concurrency-Safe Tool Batching
summary: Group tool calls into concurrency-safe batches to run read-only calls in parallel while serializing non-read-only work.
when_to_use: Turn-level toolUse collections that mix read-only calls with ones that mutate state or require exclusive execution.

name: Conversation Engine Persistent Session State
summary: Keep session-level signals (messages, read caches, memory loads) alive across turns while letting per-turn caches reset for correctness.
when_to_use: Apply when architecting a conversation engine that must persist session context while allowing per-turn caches and abort controllers to restart cleanly.

name: Create-Only Idempotent Artifact Write
summary: Persist deterministic artifacts with a create-only write and treat existing files as replay success instead of an error.
when_to_use: Apply when a deterministic artifact is keyed by a stable invocation ID and may be replayed across retries, compaction, or resume passes.

name: Cross Shell Command Safety Maps
summary: Describe how to centralize cross-platform safe commands, UNC detection, and per-shell path guards so every shell tool shares the same policy.
when_to_use: Apply this when you need to enforce identical permissions across bash/PowerShell, detect UNC paths, and keep dangerous config files hidden to both shells.

name: Dangerous Config File Protection
summary: Keep writes out of sensitive dotfiles and `.claude` folders by checking canonicalized basenames before granting permission.
when_to_use: Guard every filesystem modification that touches `.git`, `.claude`, `.gitconfig`, or other config-critical paths before running the tool.

name: Dead Path Update Skipping
summary: Skip update actions that point at missing local paths when a valid materialized entry already exists, preserving the working state instead of converting drift into a noisy failure.
when_to_use: Use when update candidates come from local paths that may disappear across checkouts, but existing materialized state should remain usable until a valid replacement exists.

name: Decision Fate Freezing
summary: Freeze each tool result ID's replacement fate after it is observed so budget decisions stay deterministic across turns and forks.
when_to_use: Apply when the message-level replacement budget runs and you need previously seen tool results to keep the same fate (persisted preview vs full content) even after resumes, forks, or cached prompts.

name: Declarative Read Only Command Allowlists
summary: Express shell-command safety as declarative flag maps so tooling knows exactly what passes.
when_to_use: Use when you want tooling to enforce read-only shell commands by referencing a static map of command names, allowed flags, and supplemental callbacks.

name: Deferred Refresh Flag Contract
summary: Decide when to auto-refresh plugins after marketplaces change and when to defer to a manual `/reload-plugins` via the `needsRefresh` flag.
when_to_use: `performBackgroundPluginInstallations` completes `reconcileMarketplaces` and must decide whether to auto-refresh plugins or set `plugins.needsRefresh` via AppState.

name: Delist-After-Reconcile Enforcement
summary: Enforce removals after extension synchronization and treat delisted entries as real downstream changes.
when_to_use: Use when an extension or plugin runtime must remove policy-blocked items after reconciliation and still propagate that change to callers.

name: Descriptive Skill Surface Admission
summary: Use source-aware admission rules so trusted local skills can surface automatically while plugin or MCP-provided entries must supply explicit descriptive metadata.
when_to_use: Use when a command or skill catalog mixes trusted local sources with external providers and you need a predictable rule for which entries may appear without manual curation.

name: Diff-First Progress Projection
summary: Project marketplace installation progress by diffing declared sources before kickoff so the UI shows pending spinners without waiting on the clone.
when_to_use: Background marketplace reconciliation needs to show progress before the clone/install work begins (before `reconcileMarketplaces`).

name: Dynamic Skill Insertion Before Builtins
summary: Merge runtime-discovered commands into an existing command registry by deduping them against the enabled base surface and inserting them at a stable boundary before built-in commands.
when_to_use: Use when commands can be discovered at runtime and must be added to a prebuilt command list without duplicating enabled entries or scrambling the established built-in ordering.

name: Empty Payload Sentinel Guard
summary: Guard requests from closing instantly when a tool returns nothing by injecting the sentinel message and logging the event.
when_to_use: Apply this when a tool_result block might legitimately be empty (shell commands with no stdout, MCP commands returning `content: []`, statements that finish silently) so the assistant still sees something to react to.

name: Empty Repo Bundle Preflight
summary: Fail fast when the repository has no refs so downstream bundling never triggers empty Git bundles.
when_to_use: Apply before running `git bundle create` to ensure the repository actually has refs, avoiding the 128 exit code and providing a clearer error path.

name: Empty Trigger Short-Circuit Before App State
summary: Skip nested memory collection when no triggers exist so render-bound `getAppState()` is never invoked unnecessarily.
when_to_use: Use when nested memory attachment triggers may be empty and you want to avoid React render-cycle work unless there is actual work to do.

name: Exact Rendered Decision Journaling
summary: Journal each per-message tool result replacement so resume logic replays the exact preview the model saw.
when_to_use: Apply whenever per-message budgeting rewrites tool_result content so transcripts capture the preview string and resume logic can reapply the same decisions.

name: External Error Preserving Refresh Merge
summary: Merge refresh-owned diagnostics with externally owned errors so one reload updates its slice without erasing adjacent subsystem failures.
when_to_use: Use when a subsystem refresh recomputes its own errors but related diagnostics from other live subsystems must remain visible.

name: Fallback Source Presence Sufficiency
summary: When a declared source is only a fallback default, treat any existing materialized source as satisfying the contract instead of forcing a replacement.
when_to_use: Apply when a configuration entry represents a fallback source and existing materialized state from another source should be preserved if it already satisfies presence.

name: Fast Worktree Reuse With Head Pointer Checks
summary: Resume existing worktrees quickly by checking stored HEAD pointers before fetching or recreating.
when_to_use: Apply when the user asks to enter or resume a named worktree and you want to avoid redundant `git fetch`/`git worktree add` calls if the worktree already exists.

name: Feature-Gated Command Registry Loading
summary: Gate optional command modules behind lazy feature checks so disabled features do not inflate startup or registry cost.
when_to_use: Use when a command registry must expose optional commands without importing their modules unless the related feature is enabled.

name: Fixed-Footprint Unicode Mascot Composition
summary: Compose terminal mascots from stable spans plus swappable fragments so animated poses stay expressive without changing layout.
when_to_use: Use when a terminal product needs mascot-style Unicode art that can animate or change expression while keeping a fixed render footprint.

name: Fork Child Directive Guardrails
summary: Keep fork-decoupled workers strictly bounded by directive text and cache sharing requirements.
when_to_use: Apply this when a forked sub-agent inherits a conversation and must obey boilerplate guard text, cache-safe output, and explicit directive rules before running.

name: Fork Gap-Fill Reconstruction
summary: Reconstruct child replacement state from local records first, then fill uncovered gaps from the parent's live map.
when_to_use: Apply when a forked or resumed child workflow must inherit prior replacement decisions even though its local transcript lacks full replacement records.

name: Forked Auto Memory Extraction
summary: Run memory harvesting in a fenced background agent after the main turn so the primary loop stays responsive.
when_to_use: Use when memory extraction should happen after the main turn in an isolated child agent instead of delaying the primary conversation loop.

name: Forked Skill Execution With Isolated Context
summary: Run SkillTool slash commands inside a forked sub-agent so the skill has isolated budget, telemetry, and lifecycle.
when_to_use: Load this skill when SkillTool needs to wrap a prompt command marked with `context:'fork'` into a sub-agent run that cannot touch the parent session state.

name: Headless Interactive Reconcile Parity
summary: Keep interactive and headless extension installation flows behaviorally aligned by sharing reconciliation logic and adapting only the outer contract.
when_to_use: Apply when the same extension installation lifecycle must work in both an interactive UI and a headless or automation mode.

name: Hook Context Attachment Injection
summary: Collect startup hook context and inject it as metadata attachments before a child agent's first request.
when_to_use: Use when startup hooks generate extra context for a spawned agent and that context must be visible before the first model call.

name: Idempotent Tool Result Persistence
summary: Persist tool results once, avoid re-writing duplicates, and summarize via previews so long outputs stay accessible yet capped.
when_to_use: Use this when tool result blocks exceed their configured thresholds and you want to persist them once while still returning a preview.

name: Inherited Flags For Spawned Teammates
summary: Propagate CLI flags/environment variables from the leader into tmux teammates so they share permission mode, plugins, and env configuration.
when_to_use: Apply whenever a teammate or tmux-based sub-agent is spawned and it must inherit the parent’s CLI flags and environment to stay aligned.

name: Initial-vs-Dynamic Skill Listing Batching
summary: Mark first-time skill listings differently from later deltas so consumers can treat bootstrap exposure and updates separately.
when_to_use: Use when a skill listing stream must distinguish the first full exposure from later incremental updates.

name: Largest First Context Shedding
summary: Reduce over-budget messages by persisting the largest fresh tool results first while leaving smaller or already-frozen blocks inline.
when_to_use: Use when one outbound message exceeds the tool-result budget and you need a deterministic rule for which fresh results to replace first.

name: Layered Command Cache Invalidation
summary: Clear every memoized layer in a command or skill pipeline so dynamic updates invalidate outer derived views as well as inner source caches.
when_to_use: Use when commands, skills, or search indexes are cached in layers and a refresh must make newly added or removed entries visible immediately.

name: Lazy Heavy Command Shims
summary: Keep heavyweight commands discoverable through lightweight shims that import the real implementation only on execution.
when_to_use: Use when command metadata should stay visible at startup but the real command implementation is too heavy to load eagerly.

name: Legacy Wire Name Compat Translation
summary: Translate renamed internal identifiers back to their legacy wire names at serialization boundaries so patch-level consumers keep working until the planned version cutoff.
when_to_use: Apply when an internal tool, command, or capability identifier has been renamed, but existing SDK or UI consumers still depend on the old wire name and cannot safely break on a patch release.

name: Lifecycle-Scoped Resource Cleanup
summary: Release every resource allocated to a child agent inside one unconditional cleanup path so finished or aborted runs leave no lingering hooks, watchers, or background jobs.
when_to_use: Each agent run, regardless of outcome, must unwind resources reserved for its lifetime without assuming the parent session will later re-claim them.

name: Local-and-MCP Skill Name Deduplication
summary: Merge local and MCP skill sources into one listing surface and dedupe by stable command name before announcing them.
when_to_use: Apply when a listing surface combines commands from multiple registries or providers and must avoid announcing the same user-facing capability twice.

name: Main Agent Vs Background Memory Extraction Exclusion
summary: Skip the background memory extractor when the main agent already persisted memory during the same turn to avoid duplicates and race hazards.
when_to_use: Apply whenever the background memory agent must determine whether the parent already saved the same memories so it can safely skip processing.

name: Main-Thread Empty-String Agent Key Normalization
summary: Normalize the main thread to a concrete empty-string key so agent-scoped state maps treat main and forked threads uniformly.
when_to_use: Apply when thread-scoped state is keyed by agent identity, but the main thread has no explicit agent ID and still needs stable map behavior.

name: Main Thread Only Cache Editing
summary: Gate cached microcompact cache edits to the main thread so background forks cannot alter the shared cache state.
when_to_use: Use when cached microcompact runs and you must prevent subagents or forks from writing cache edits that the main thread shares.

name: Mark-After-Filter Memory Deduplication
summary: Filter prefetched memory attachments against cumulative read state before mutating that state, so async surfacing does not dedupe itself away.
when_to_use: Apply when asynchronously prefetched memories or file attachments must be deduplicated against a cumulative read-state cache that they will also update after surviving the filter.

name: Materialization-Aware Refresh Escalation
summary: React to marketplace materialization diffs by refreshing plugins when new installs land and escalating to needsRefresh when updates or refresh failures occur.
when_to_use: When background plugin installation reconcilers detect that declared marketplaces differ from what is materialized, use the diff to decide whether to auto-refresh plugins or escalate to a manual refresh prompt.

name: MCP Skill vs Prompt Discovery Filtering
summary: Merge MCP-discovered prompt skills with local commands only after type filtering and name deduplication.
when_to_use: Use when MCP-discovered commands should appear in a unified skill surface without shadowing or duplicating local prompt commands.

name: Media-Block Compaction Prep
summary: Replace heavy media blocks with lightweight placeholders before compaction so summaries stay inside budget without losing structural cues.
when_to_use: Use when compaction must summarize a session that contains images, documents, or other heavy user-supplied media blocks.

name: Memoized Command Source Aggregation
summary: Load independent command sources in parallel, then memoize the merged registry by cwd so repeated lookups stay cheap and ordered.
when_to_use: Use when a command or skill registry must combine several expensive sources, but repeated lookups from the same workspace should reuse one stable merged result.

name: Memory Directory Exists Guidance
summary: Ensure memory directories exist once and surface consistent guidance so agents skip redundant mkdir calls.
when_to_use: Use whenever you plan to write memory files so the agent can assume the directory exists instead of repeatedly running `mkdir -p`.

name: Memory Entrypoint With Index Discipline
summary: Keep MEMORY.md terse by capping entries, warning when caps hit, and pointing each memory to its own file.
when_to_use: Use whenever you’re writing to MEMORY.md so the index stays readable and never exceeds terminal-safe length budgets.

name: Modality-Preserving Binary Offload
summary: Persist binary output with a mime-derived extension and a matching saved-file message so downstream tools keep the right modality.
when_to_use: Apply when a tool emits binary content that must be saved out of band without losing the file type or downstream usability.

name: Mode-Gated Reconcile Skip Filter
summary: Inject mode-specific skip rules into a shared reconciler so unsupported source types are recorded and skipped without forking the core install logic.
when_to_use: Apply when a shared reconcile engine runs in multiple runtime modes, but some modes cannot support every source type or transport.

name: Namespace-Resolved Skill Preloading
summary: Resolve startup skill names through layered namespace lookup before preloading them as explicit visible context.
when_to_use: Use when agent definitions preload startup skills by friendly name but the registry may store them under namespaced or plugin-qualified identifiers.

name: Nested Memory Trigger Consume and Clear
summary: Process every nested-memory trigger before wiping the trigger set so subsequent iterations only see new paths.
when_to_use: Apply when a worker must fetch nested memory attachments whose trigger list can be cleared only after all paths are handled.

name: No-Op Empty Skill Delta Return
summary: Return no attachment when an incremental skill delta has no unseen items instead of emitting a formally empty update payload.
when_to_use: Apply when an incremental attachment or announcement pass recomputes a delta set and discovers there is nothing new to send.

name: Nonblocking Plugin Install Status Projection
summary: Map async marketplace reconciliation events into AppState rows so UIs show install/installing/failed badges without blocking.
when_to_use: Use when marketplace reconciliation emits progress events but the main UI must remain responsive with up-to-date status rows.

name: Nonblocking Post-Connect Capability Broadcast
summary: Mark bridge sessions ready first, then send optional capability metadata asynchronously without delaying readiness.
when_to_use: Use when a remote bridge or transport should become usable immediately while optional capability discovery continues in the background.

name: Null-Hardened Feature Overrides
summary: Validate remote numeric override maps defensively so null, non-finite, or negative flag payloads never corrupt runtime thresholds.
when_to_use: Apply when remote feature flags or runtime override maps can influence thresholds, but the flag service may return null or malformed values.

name: Ordered Streaming Tool Execution
summary: Buffer and execute streaming tool calls with concurrency awareness so results appear in request order even when multiple runners overlap.
when_to_use: Streaming toolUse input where progress must be shown in the original queue order, even if some tools run concurrently.

name: Orphaned Tool Call Sanitizer
summary: Remove assistant messages that reference tools without recorded results before feeding the history back into runAgent.
when_to_use: When replaying history for a subagent, make sure assistant tool_use blocks never refer to tool_use_ids that lack results.

name: Parallelism-Aware Skill Step Design
summary: Design skill steps with explicit execution topology, asking which parts run directly, in subagents, in teammate swarms, or in parallel.
when_to_use: Use when converting a workflow into a skill and some steps may run in parallel, through task agents, through teammate swarms, or require human checkpoints.

name: Per-Agent Skill Listing Dedupe
summary: Track announced skills per agent or thread so each child agent gets its own first-turn capability listing instead of inheriting another thread's seen state.
when_to_use: Use when multiple agents or threads can receive skill listings and each one needs its own delta tracking instead of a shared global seen-set.

name: Permission Overlay Inheritance
summary: Layer child permission rules over parent session policy without dropping root-level grants or mutating shared state.
when_to_use: Apply when a subagent or nested workflow needs a narrower permission contract but must still inherit the parent session's top-level grants.

name: Plan Mode Permission Gating
summary: Enter plan mode through an explicit permission transition that enforces a no-edit planning state before implementation.
when_to_use: Use when complex tasks should move the session into a reviewed planning mode before file edits or implementation work begin.

name: Plugin-Only Agent Surface Gating
summary: Prevent untrusted agent definitions from expanding the MCP surface when the session is locked to plugin-only extensions.
when_to_use: Use when agent-defined MCP servers must respect a plugin-only trust boundary instead of silently expanding the runtime surface.

name: Portable Skill Loader Progressive Disclosure
summary: Keep the universal skill compact yet discoverable by loading minimal frontmatter first and heavy content on demand.
when_to_use: Use when an agent must scan thousands of skills but only load detailed prompts for the handful that are actually invoked.

name: Pose-Fragment Unicode Sprite Design
summary: Build expressive Unicode sprite systems from a stable base plus localized fragment swaps so poses stay aligned and cheap to extend.
when_to_use: Use when a terminal character or icon needs multiple expressions or gestures while preserving one stable silhouette and spacing model.

name: Prevent Continuation Stop-Hook Contract
summary: Return a narrow stop-hook result that can halt the query loop without throwing, while still surfacing blocking errors separately.
when_to_use: Apply when post-response hooks may decide that the query loop must stop, but the caller still needs a structured non-exception contract.

name: Prevent Continuation Stop Hook Messaging
summary: Collect stop-hook progress/errors, emit a structured prevented-continuation attachment, and summarize the block for users and logs.
when_to_use: Use when stop hooks prevent continuation so you can surface a consistent attachment plus a summary notification that downstream UI/logging consumes.

name: Preview-First Large Output Messages
summary: Persist oversized tool output once and lead with a preview plus saved-artifact reference so readers see the start without losing the full result.
when_to_use: Use when tool or task output is too large to inline and the response should preserve the full artifact while still showing a bounded preview from the beginning.

name: Privacy-Redacted REPL Bridge System Init
summary: Reuse one `system/init` payload shape across trusted and remote transports, but redact tool, MCP, and plugin metadata when the bridge path would leak local integrations or filesystem paths.
when_to_use: Use when the same session-init or capability payload is emitted to both trusted SDK/internal consumers and remote transports that may persist or forward the metadata.

name: Progress Event State Bridge
summary: Map background reconciliation progress events onto shared UI state so each source row reflects installing, installed, or failed.
when_to_use: Use when a background install or reconciliation engine emits per-source progress events and the UI needs one immutable path to reflect those transitions.

name: Progress-First Tool Emission
summary: Emit progress/value updates as soon as they arrive while still buffering final results and handling cancellations or sibling errors.
when_to_use: Streaming tool runners that produce progress messages or status updates alongside final tool results.

name: Progressive Compaction Prompt
summary: Drive compaction summaries through structured prompts that sequence naming, artifacts, and invocation guidance before stripping analysis tags.
when_to_use: Apply when composing compact summaries so each round captures intent, context, per-step detail, and invocation cues before the final summary is stripped to user-friendly text.

name: Progressive Width Gated Status Rows
summary: Show status metadata only when the spinner row has room, shrinking entries as viewport width decreases.
when_to_use: Apply this when terminal status rows need to show token counters, timers, and thinking text but must drop elements as available width shrinks.

name: Project-Root Scoped Local Skill Loading
summary: Resolve a stable project root first, then load local skills relative to that scope before combining them with other sources.
when_to_use: Apply when local commands or skills must be discovered from the current workspace and later merged with other registries such as MCP or bundled sources.

name: Prompt-Context Handoff for Agent Runtime
summary: Package prompt, identity, and runtime constraints into one explicit handoff so child agents start with the right context and no accidental leakage.
when_to_use: Use when a parent agent or workflow must launch a child agent with a prompt plus selected runtime state, constraints, and capabilities.

name: Reachable WIP Via Temporary Refs
summary: Capture in-flight WIP via `git stash create`, make it reachable through temporary refs, and tear down the refs after bundling finishes.
when_to_use: Apply when bundling or seeding a repository and you want to include uncommitted work without disturbing the working tree or leaving dangling refs.

name: Reduced-Motion First Terminal Animation
summary: Pause terminal animations when the user requests reduced motion, then resume cleanly without tearing the rest of the UI.
when_to_use: Build any animation that should stay dormant until the user explicitly opts into motion.

name: Reinjected Attachment Pruning Before Summary
summary: Prune attachments that will be regenerated after compaction so the summarizer spends budget on durable context instead.
when_to_use: Use when some attachment types are guaranteed to be reintroduced after compaction and should not consume summary tokens twice.

name: Relevant Memory Selection By Header
summary: Rank memory files by header descriptions so only a small high-signal subset is injected back into context.
when_to_use: Use when many memory files exist and the runtime needs to pick only the most relevant few for the current query.

name: Remote Safe Command Allowlist
summary: Define a narrow explicit set of commands that stay available in remote mode because they only affect local UI state, then reuse that set at startup and after handshake refinement.
when_to_use: Use when a remote session can expose a subset of local commands, but only commands with no filesystem, shell, IDE, or other host-bound dependencies should remain available.

name: Required Argument Aware Flag Validation
summary: Walk command tokens while inspecting attached/inline arguments so validators never skip missing or dangerous flag values.
when_to_use: Apply this whenever flag validation cannot rely on shell parsers and must check `--flag=value`, combined short bundles, and missing arguments explicitly.

name: Reserved Summary Output Budget
summary: Reserve part of the context window for summary output so proactive compaction does not kill the response budget.
when_to_use: Apply when proving that proactive compaction must shrink the usable context window to keep the summary output under a known token budget.

name: Resume Skill Listing Suppression
summary: Mark the current skill listing as already sent when resuming so the transcript does not receive a redundant payload.
when_to_use: Apply when a resume flow already contains a previous `skill_listing` attachment and injecting another would waste ~600 tokens.

name: Role-Aware Context Slimming
summary: Trim user and system context based on the agent’s role so read-only helpers don’t inherit stale git or CLAUDE metadata.
when_to_use: When an agent declares `omitClaudeMd` or runs as an Explore/Plan helper, slim the context before handing it to `runAgent`.

name: Safe Worktree Slug Validation
summary: Ensure Git worktree slugs can never escape the managed workspace or inject traversal.
when_to_use: Apply this when accepting user-provided prefixes for `EnterWorktree` and you need to guarantee no path traversal or invalid chars slip into the worktree directory.

name: Scrollback Freeze Optimization
summary: Cache and reuse animated children once they slide into scrollback so terminal resets never occur.
when_to_use: Apply whenever you render active widgets that can scroll into terminal history and would otherwise force full-screen updates.

name: Seed-Before-Diff Marketplace Registration
summary: Register seeded extension sources before diffing so bootstrap content is treated as already present.
when_to_use: Use when startup includes pre-seeded sources or mirrors that should participate in reconciliation without being recloned.

name: Semantic Theme-Key Rendering
summary: Resolve semantic color keys through the active palette and contextual overrides before rendering terminal text.
when_to_use: Use when terminal components accept theme keys such as warning, success, or muted rather than hardcoded color literals.

name: Session Memory First Compaction
summary: Try lightweight session-memory summaries before falling back to heavyweight full-conversation compaction.
when_to_use: Use when auto-compaction has access to cached session summaries that can often replace a full transcript compaction pass.

name: Session Memory Mailbox Exclusion
summary: Prevent session-memory background forks from draining teammate mailbox attachments that belong to the main lead thread.
when_to_use: Apply when a background or forked agent shares team context with a leader thread and must not consume mailbox messages intended for the leader.

name: Shared System Init Shape Parity
summary: Centralize `system/init` or capability payload construction in one builder so multiple transports emit identical schemas and only vary inputs or delivery.
when_to_use: Use when the same `system/init` or capability-announcement payload is emitted from multiple entrypoints or transports and downstream clients depend on one stable schema.

name: Skill Listing Reset on Real Skill Changes
summary: Reset skill-listing announcement state only when the underlying skill set actually changes, not on compaction or transcript churn.
when_to_use: Apply when a system tracks which skills have already been announced and must decide when that sent-state should be cleared.

name: Skill Registry Composition and Feature Gating
summary: Compose skill registries in fixed phases with explicit feature gates so merged command surfaces stay deterministic and cheap to refresh.
when_to_use: Use when skills or prompt commands come from multiple sources and optional features must control which sources load and in what order.

name: Skill-Scoped Permission Suggestions
summary: Suggest the narrowest permission scope by mapping a file path to its enclosing skill directory before prompting the user.
when_to_use: Apply this when Claude is asking for filesystem permission that can be narrowed to a single skill directory.

name: Skip Skill Discovery on Skill Expansion
summary: Bypass skill discovery when expanded SKILL.md or generated prompt content is being reprocessed for attachments, so non-user text does not trigger recursive discovery work.
when_to_use: Apply when generated or expanded skill text must pass through the attachment pipeline for parsing, but it is not fresh user intent and must not trigger discovery queries.

name: Skip Skill Listing in Test Environment
summary: Suppress skill-listing attachments in test mode so discoverability noise does not interfere with deterministic test behavior.
when_to_use: Apply when tests exercise attachment-building code paths but do not need discoverability payloads such as skill listings.

name: Skip Skill Listing Without Skill Tool
summary: Omit skill-listing attachments for agents that do not expose the Skill tool, so discoverability context is only sent where it can be acted on.
when_to_use: Apply when an attachment or context injector is about to advertise capabilities to an agent that may not have the tool needed to use them.

name: Slash-Command Skill Classification Filter
summary: Derive a slash-command skill surface from a broader prompt-command registry by requiring descriptive metadata, excluding builtins, and admitting only a controlled set of origins plus explicit user-only commands.
when_to_use: Use when a slash-command picker or human-facing skill list must be derived from a larger prompt-command registry without exposing builtin, undocumented, or wrongly sourced commands.

name: Staged Bundle Fallback Chain
summary: Retry Git bundle creation across decreasing scopes (all, HEAD, squashed) to stay under upload size limits while keeping relevant history.
when_to_use: Apply when you are creating a Git bundle for upload and you need a staged fallback chain from full history to a minimal seed that still fits the byte ceiling.

name: Stall-to-Alert Color Escalation
summary: Blend progress colors toward an alert hue as stall intensity rises so stuck work becomes obvious without changing layout.
when_to_use: Use when spinners or progress indicators should communicate rising stall severity through color instead of larger warning UI.

name: Steady-State Sidecar Sync
summary: Rebuild derived sidecar caches from authoritative extension state even when the current run made no new installs.
when_to_use: Use when mirrored caches or sidecar artifacts can disappear between runs even though the primary installed set stayed unchanged.

name: Subagent Metrics Bridging
summary: Connect async subagent streams to progress UI, analytics, and notifications from one finalized lifecycle snapshot.
when_to_use: Use when background or async subagents must report progress, token usage, completion, and partial results back to the host runtime.

name: Tail-Preserving Task Output Truncation
summary: Persist tool outputs alongside metadata and truncate only the preview, ensuring the header points back to the persisted replica.
when_to_use: Use this when a task produces output larger than the session’s configured limit and you need to keep a concise preview plus a persistent archive.

name: Telemetry Sanitized Skill Invocation
summary: Record SkillTool usage with sanitized telemetry fields so built-in, bundled, and custom skills are distinguished while avoiding PII leakage.
when_to_use: Use when SkillTool emits `tengu_skill_tool_invocation` so built-in commands, remote MCP skills, and forked executions are logged without exposing command strings in open analytics tables.

name: Terminal-specific Art Fallbacks
summary: Layer terminal detection and fallback fragments so the same mascot identity renders correctly across Apple Terminal, xterm, and other hosts.
when_to_use: When the same brand art must render on Apple Terminal, xterm, and other hosts with different Unicode/color capabilities while preserving the mascot identity.

name: Three-Layer Extension Sync
summary: Model extension lifecycle as intent, materialization, and active runtime layers, then sync each layer with dedicated transitions.
when_to_use: Apply when extensions exist simultaneously as declared settings, on-disk installs, and live runtime components, and those layers must stay coherent.

name: Time-Based Microcompact Trigger
summary: Fall back to time-based microcompaction when the idle gap exceeds the configured threshold and directly clear the oldest compactable tool results.
when_to_use: Apply when the interval since the last assistant response exceeds the threshold and cached microcompact cannot run.

name: Time-Gap Cache-Expiry Microcompact
summary: When a long idle gap means the server cache is cold, clear old tool-result content before the next request instead of attempting warm-cache cache edits.
when_to_use: Apply when a long gap since the last assistant response implies the prompt cache has expired and cache-editing no longer makes sense.

name: Token Budgeted Compaction With Reinjection
summary: Keep compaction budgets under control by compacting incrementally and reinjecting truncated attachments only when the reasoning surface remains stable.
when_to_use: Use when session history is growing toward the API limit and you need a safe way to trim older context while still reinjecting critical artifacts like skill metadata.

name: Tool Success Aware Memory Suppression
summary: Hide context docs for tools that already succeeded this turn while keeping guidance available for failed or unresolved tools.
when_to_use: Use when memory or doc injection should focus on the tools the model is struggling with instead of re-explaining tools it already used successfully in the current turn.

name: Transport Error Normalization and Retry Policy
summary: Normalize transport failures into one retry policy so streaming bridges, sockets, and remote clients fail predictably instead of improvising recovery.
when_to_use: Use when a remote or bridge transport can fail in multiple ways and callers need one consistent rule for retrying, surfacing, or terminating.

name: Trusted Frontmatter Gating
summary: Gate frontmatter MCP servers, hooks, and skills behind admin trust when plugin-only or hook-only modes are active.
when_to_use: Apply this pattern when you are about to honor MCP servers, hooks, or skill preloads declared in an agent’s frontmatter while the session may be locked to plugin-only/customized trust settings.

name: Turn-Scoped Memory Prefetch with Disposal
summary: Start a non-blocking, turn-scoped memory prefetch that auto-disposes and only injects results once per turn.
when_to_use: When a turn needs contextual memory attachments but the main loop must stay streaming and reuse previous read state.

name: Turn Scoped Skill Discovery Reset
summary: Reset per-turn skill discovery tracking so long-lived sessions don’t leak outdated discovery flags while still honoring already-known skills.
when_to_use: Use when the conversation engine needs to clear the list of discovered skills at the start of each new turn so repeated invocations stay accurate.

name: Turn-Zero vs Inter-Turn Skill Discovery Split
summary: Block only the first discovery pass, then overlap later discovery with tool execution so streaming turns stay responsive.
when_to_use: Use when first-turn skill discovery must be accurate but repeated blocking discovery would slow every later turn.

name: Type Tiered Bridge Command Gating
summary: Gate bridge-executed slash commands by command type first: block local-jsx UI commands, allow prompt commands by construction, and require an explicit allowlist for plain local commands.
when_to_use: Use when a remote or bridged client can invoke slash commands and you need a simple safety rule that separates text-expanding commands from local UI commands and plain local actions.

name: User Input Before Thread Attachments
summary: Resolve user-input-driven attachments first, then run later attachment builders that depend on derived triggers or harvested metadata.
when_to_use: Use when an attachment pipeline mixes direct user-input parsing with second-stage builders that rely on triggers collected during that first pass.

name: User-Invocable Capability Serialization
summary: Serialize announced command and skill surfaces from a shared capability shape, excluding entries explicitly marked non-user-invocable.
when_to_use: Use when a session-init payload or capability announcement is projecting commands, skills, or similar affordances into a user-visible surface and some registry entries are internal-only.

name: Viewport-Aware Animation Pausing
summary: Stop updating animation ticks as soon as the animated element leaves the viewport so scrollback never forces terminal resets.
when_to_use: Deploy animations that pause as soon as their viewport flags `isVisible` false.

name: Wire-Shape Aligned Budgeting
summary: Keep each wire message’s aggregate tool_result size within the configured budget by compacting the largest fresh results first.
when_to_use: Apply this when building user messages that include multiple `tool_result` blocks to ensure the total stays under `MAX_TOOL_RESULTS_PER_MESSAGE_CHARS` before the API call.

name: Withheld Max Output Token Errors
summary: Delay surfacing max-output-token failures until the recovery loop completes, then emit sanitized tool_result errors.
when_to_use: Use this when a user turn hits the max-output-token limit but the system keeps retrying so the recovery loop should finish before reporting an error.

name: Wrapped Text Style Preservation
summary: Track per-character segments so wrapped or truncated text keeps its original colors and emphasis.
when_to_use: When you must wrap or truncate a styled text run but cannot afford to lose per-segment colors, links, or emphasis, especially inside terminals that split lines at wrap boundaries.
